title: [D1] Spectral Decomposition of Iterative Teacher Residues
content: To arbitrate the teacher-ladder fight between single-pass RTS and iterative teacher^2, we must test whether multi-pass distillation extracts genuine non-Markovian micro-structure or merely fits backward-pass interpolation noise. Construct a synthetic test harness using our 1hz market tick recordings where the true underlying drift is known. Train Student A on single-pass RTS smoothing targets. Train Student B on iterative teacher^2 targets. Compare their out-of-sample log-loss against the known ground truth across high and low volatility regimes. If teacher^2 only improves variance fitting in high-volatility windows while degrading calibration in quiet regimes, it is confirming noise laundering.
mechanism: Iterative teachers apply sequential backward smoothing passes that amplify non-linear gradient signals. However, without a bounding function, this amplification risks mapping sample-specific noise as structural edge.
test: Regress Student A and Student B OOS Brier scores against hidden ground-truth drift in synthetic 1hz windows. Kill condition: If Student B's log-loss is higher than Student A's in low-volatility regimes, iterative teacher^2 is noise laundering, confirming single-pass RTS as the structural ceiling.
